methods.description: The method involves analyzing pollen grain diameters and their respective averages and confidence intervals to identify distinct groups and summarize data variability using PCA.

steps.description: The quantitative data analyses used pollen grain diameters and their respective averages and confidence intervals.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze pollen grain diameters and calculate averages and confidence intervals

steps.description: We observed three distinct groups when analysing the metric values of the diameters.
steps.searchable_summary: identify distinct groups based on metric values of diameters

steps.description: The PCA summarised 78.82% of the total variability of the data, in which axis 1 was more informative to the PCA since it summarised 43.47% of the variability.
steps.searchable_summary: perform PCA to summarize data variability

steps.description: The analysed Mascagnia and Ectopopterys were recovered far from those of Amorimia.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze species positioning in PCA results

steps.description: For axis 1, the most significant variable was ECLEN, which distinguished Ectopopterys from other species by the colporate ectoaperture.
steps.searchable_summary: identify significant variables for PCA axis 1

steps.description: Axis 2 summarised 31.34% of the variability in our data; the most significant variables for this axis were SEXI and ECWID.
steps.searchable_summary: identify significant variables for PCA axis 2

steps.description: Note that the species of Mascagnia and Ectopopterys had lower values for these variables and were close to A. amazonica.
steps.searchable_summary: compare species values for significant variables
